title: Diarrhena maritima
intro:
There is no information available on Diarrhena maritima. However, I found information on Diarrhena obovata, also known as American Brome or Wood Brome, which is a plant in the same genus.

For Diarrhena obovata, it is a perennial grass native to North America. The health benefits of Diarrhena obovata are not well-documented, but it has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Diarrhena obovata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The most common herbal preparations made with Diarrhena obovata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat digestive issues and other health conditions.
